ety: khap˚ = Prk. खप्प(इ) = S. क्षप्य(ते), fr. क्षप्, caus. of क्षि
def: v.n. To be destroyed, be wiped out, be disposed of, be killed or slain; to be made away with, to be swallowed up; to be consumed; to be expended, to be used, to be exhausted; to be lost (in, -meṅ); to be absorbed (by, -meṅ), to be dried up; — to obtain a vent or market, to find sale, to go off, to sell; — to be fitted (into); to fit or go (into); to enter, penetrate (into, -meṅ); to be mixed up (with, -meṅ); to mix (with), join or unite (with); to remain; — s.m. A company (of travellers, &c.); a crowd
more: v. n. 1. To be absorbed ( sukhnā ). Ser bhar āṭe meṅ ser bhar ghī khap gayā . 2. To be used, expended, consumed. Sārā rapayā khap gayā . 3. To be mixed up with; to pass muster. Achchhoṅ meṅ bare bhī khap jāte haiṅ . 4. To be killed or slain ( kām ānā , 3). Is laṛāī meṅ bhī hazāroṅ ādmī khap gaë! 5. To go into; to fit ( samānā ).
1. v. n. To be dried up or absorbed. 2. To sell, to go off, to be expended or made away with. 3. To remain, to join, to mix with, to enter, to penetrate. 4. s. m. A company (of travellers, &c.)

A vessel in which the blood of a victim is offered. khappar bharnā , v. n. 1. To make a bloodoffering. 2. To give alms to a jogī in the name of Shiv.
s. m. 1. The skull, the cranium. 2. An earthen cup used by jogīs. 3. The vessel in which the blood of a victim is offered. 4. A chafingdish.
subs: (-kā), 'To fill a Jogī's or mendicant's dish,' to give alms (to a Jogī, in the name of Śiva); to make a blood-offering.

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَارِش (xāriš).

ety: abst. s. fr. ḵẖārīdan; see ḵẖār
def: s.f. The itch; scab; mange.
more: n. f. P. خاريدن to itch. The itch ( khujlī ); scabe; mange.
s. f. The itch, the scab.

def: adj. Itchy; scabby; mangy.
s.m. See s.v. ḵẖār. s.m. A thorn, prickle, spine; thistle, bramble; a spur, a cock's spur; — bristle, hair
subs: Anything disagreeable to the sight, an eye-sore
s.f. A fence, or temporary defence, made of thorns
adj. Thorny, prickly, bristling; barbed; troublesome, arduous; — having a beard, bearded
s.f. = ḵẖār-istān, s.m. A place of thorns, thistles, &c.; a thicket, a brake
(-par), To feel or have a thorn in the flesh; to feel jealous or envious (of)
v.n. To be a thorn in the side, be an eye-sore, be disagreeable (to).

ety: for A. خريطة ḵẖarīt̤at, fem.; fr. خرط 'to pull off,' &c.
def: s.m. A receptacle, pouch, purse (of leather, &c.); a hold-all; a letter-bag; a small silk bag in which letters for great men are enclosed; — (met.) a letter (from one great personage to another).
more: n. m. خرط made fast. 1. A receptacle; a small silk bag in which letters for great men are enclosed; a letter bag. 2. ( Met .) A letter from one ruler to another. 3. A hold-all. 4. A pocket.

s. m. A purse, packet, bag, mail. (Letters are sent in India enclosed in bags, diversified according to the quality of the person to whom sent, being more or less rich or embroidered agreeable to their rank). Met. A letter.

def: s.f. Chalk; a piece of chalk ( = khaṛī, q.v.); a chalk-mark. s.f. = خواري ḵẖẉārī, q.v. s.f. Contemptibleness, meanness, baseness, vileness; abjectness, friendlessness, wretchedness, distress.
more: Māṛ n. f. S. खटिका Chalk; white clay; pipe clay; a species of steatites with which children learn to write, and which is used in giving a polish to stucco. khaṛyā potnā, phernā , v. a. To whitewash. khallī-chhūnā , E. v. a. lit . to touch the chalk. To take one's first lesson.

ety: Prk. खडिआ; S. खडिका, or खटिका
def: s.f. Chalk; white elay, pipe-clay; a species of steatites (with which children learn to write, and which is used in giving a polish to stucco); — a chalk-mark
more: s. f. Chalk. See کهڙي khaṛī.
subs: (-meṅ), To apply chalk or whitening (to); to pipe-clay
or kaṭ-khaṛī, s.f. A species of steatites ( = khaṛī); a pencil made of the same.

def: v.t. = khaliyānā, q.v. v.t. To skin, flay.
s.f. Asinine quality or nature; stupidity; obstinacy.
s. (dakh.) A dish prepared of eggs, &c. ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

ety: inf. n. of خرج 'to go out or forth,' &c.
def: s.m. What goes out or is expended (of a man's property, &c.), outgoings, &c.; see ḵẖarć, the com. form.
more: s. m. Expenditure, spending, disbursement, expense, price, outgoing. (Plur. اخراجات). خرچ اخراجات ḵẖarch iḵẖrājāt, Expenditure. خرچ کرنا ḵẖarch karnā, To expend, disburse, spend. خرچ کودينا ḵẖarch ko denā (dakh. i. q. مارنا) To kill (?). خرچ هونا ḵẖarch honā, To be expended.

def: s.f. = kharj, q.v. s.m. What goes out or is expended (of a man's property, &c.), outgoings, &c.; see ḵẖarć, the com. form.
s.f. The bass (in Music).
more: n. f. The bass. Kharaj meṅ gāo .

def: v. a. (dakh.) To expend: see خرچنا ḵẖarach-nā.
itching
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit खर्जु (kharju). Doublet of کُھجْلِی (khujlī).

def: s.m. See khajūr.

ety: inf. n. of خرق 'to bore or cut through,' &c.
def: s.m. Tearing, rending, breaking through, infringing (used in comp.)
subs: s.f. An event breaking through or infringing the usual course (of nature), an unusual or extraordinary thing, a miracle (pl. ḵẖarq-ě-ʻādāt, miracles).

def: s.m. = khaṛkā, q.v.
more: n. m. S. खड़क 1. ( khuṛkā ) Clatter; rattle. 2. A tooth-pick; a stalk used as a pin to keep leaves together. Ban ke pāt, ban ke khaṛikā, Kerī karat Bārī ke larikā . Bhoj. Prov. With forest leaves and stalks for toys, Amuse themselves leaf-joiner's boys.

def: s.m. A toothpick; — a stalk used as a pin to keep leaves together.
more: (prop. khaṭkā ) s. m. 1. Doubt, apprehension. 2. A toothpick.

def: s.m. = khaṭkā, q.v. s.m. Knocking, rapping, rattling (of a stick, &c.); knock, rap, rattling sound or noise, clatter; sound of footsteps; — a rattle, clapper (to scare away birds = khaṭkhaṭā); a bolt, catch; — a pricking or rankling (in the mind); offence; a catch or hitch (in the mind), scruple, demur, misgiving, suspicion, doubt, suspense; perturbation, anxiety, care, concern; apprehension, fear, dread; — loss (in trade, &c.; in this sense generally khuṭkā); — a shake, or trill (in music, or in a voice)
subs: (-ko), To have a misgiving; to feel apprehension, to be apprehensive, to fear, &c.
(-kā), To listen for the sound of footsteps
adv. Without scruple or misgiving; without apprehension or fear; unhesitatingly; smoothly, easily.

def: s. f. (dakh.) Apprehension, suspicion, doubt.
v.t. = khaṛkānā, q.v.
more: v. a. 1. Caus. of khaṛaknā , q. v. 2. To scare or frighten away.
v. a. 1. To thump, to shake. 2. To frighten away, to put to flight. 3. To sell.

def: v.t. To knock or rap at, to thump, to rattle or shake (a chain, &c.), — to slam (a door, &c.); — to frighten away, to scare (by rattling, or other like noise); — to warn, to apprize (by rapping, or rattling, &c.).
s.f. Chalk; a piece of chalk ( = khaṛī, q.v.). s.f. = خواري ḵẖẉārī, q.v. s.f. Contemptibleness, meanness, baseness, vileness; abjectness, friendlessness, wretchedness, distress.

ety: = S.खर redupl.; — or fr. A. خرّ 'to sound'
def: s.m. Snoring; purring (of a cat); gurgling or rattling (of the throat, as in choking, or in croup, &c.)
more: n. m. 1. Snoring. 2. The purring of a cat. 3. Hawking up phlegm from the throat.
Purring (of a cat). خرخرکرنا ḵẖar ḵẖar karnā, To purr.

def: s.f. = khaṛkhaṛāhaṭ.
s.m. Snoring, &c. ( = ḵẖar-ḵẖar)
adj. (f. -ī), Rough, rugged; uneven; — s.m. A currycomb.
more: s. m. A currycomb. ¶ khurkhurā, adj. Rough.

def: s.f. = khaṛkhaṛāhaṭ, q.v.
s.f. Creaking, jarring (as of a door); clashing, clanking (of two bodies), rattling; snoring; clash, clang, clatter, rattle.
more: (from کهڙکهڙانا) s. f. Creaking, clatter, rattling, jarring (as of a door), snoring.

def: n. f. Clatter; rattle; clang.
v.t. = khaṛkhaṛānā, q.v.
more: v. a. See khaṛaknā. Bāṅke bhī ho-ke ham ne is dīd ko uṛāyā, Shamshīr aur sipar ko ek umr khaṛ-khaṛāyā . Nazir. A gallant gay I've toyed with maid and wife, And sword and lance I've brandished all my life. 2. See āṛe hāth lenā 2, -6. Āj unko bhī khaṛkhaṛā ḍālā .

ety: khaṛkhaṛ˚ = S. स्खल redupl.; — cf. S. खडुखुडाय
def: v.t. To make a noise or clatter with; to rattle; to shake, flourish, brandish (a sword, &c.); — to grind (the teeth); to chide, rate, scold, upbraid, rebuke; — v.n. To creak, clatter, rattle, jar, clash; — to snore
more: v. a. 1. To creak, to clatter, to rattle, to jar. 2. To grind the teeth. 3. To snore.
subs: v.t. (intens.) To shake, rattle; — to scold roundly, to rate or chide severely.

def: v. (dakh.) To snore.
s.f. = khaṛkhaṛāhaṭ, q.v.
s.f. = khaṛkhaṛiyā, q.v.
more: s. f. A chair, a litter.

ety: khaṛ-khaṛ, q.v.+iyā = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A kind of palanquin, a litter; — a break; a rattling or rickety vehicle, a rattletrap.
s.m. A crowd, tumult, riot; a dispute, quarrel; causeless wrangling; encounter; plea; litigation; — mental distress, agitation, anxiety (syn. jhagṛā; andesha).
more: s. m. A tumult, crowd, riot, quarrelling, encounter.

def: s.m. See s.v. ḵẖar. s.m. A thorn, prickle, spine; thistle, bramble; a spur, a cock's spur; — bristle, hair
more: s. m. Master, owner, possessor. (Spoken contemptuously).
subs: Anything disagreeable to the sight, an eye-sore
s.f. A fence, or temporary defence, made of thorns
adj. Thorny, prickly, bristling; barbed; troublesome, arduous; — having a beard, bearded
s.f. = ḵẖār-istān, s.m. A place of thorns, thistles, &c.; a thicket, a brake
(-par), To feel or have a thorn in the flesh; to feel jealous or envious (of)
v.n. To be a thorn in the side, be an eye-sore, be disagreeable (to).

def: n. f. A young heifer bearing her first calf.
s. f. Contrary to nature, a miracle. (Plur. خرق عادات).
s.f. A squabble, wrangling.
adj. 1. Athletick. 2. Stupid.
s.m. The small shell called 'cowrie' (kauṛī), Cyprœa moneta.
s. m. The small shell called kauṛī, used as coin. (Cypræa moneta).
s.m. A species of large rat, a bandicoot (syn. ghūs).
more: s. A kind of large rat. See H. گهوس ghūs, sign. 1.

def: n. m. Caste; breed (of cattle).
adj. = P خروار ḵẖar-wār, s.m. See s.v. ḵẖar.
s.f. Scratching, scraping; scrapings (of a pot).
v.t. To scrape, scratch, to claw.
more: v. a. To scrape, to scratch.

def: v.t. = kharoćnā, q.v. v.t. To scrape, scratch, to claw.
s.f. Scratching, clawing; a scratch (with the nail or claw); a graze
more: s. f. A scratch with the nail.
subs: v.n. To receive a scratch, or claw; to be scratched, be grazed.

def: v.t. To scratch, &c. ( = kharoćnā, q.v.). v.t. To scrape, scratch, to claw.

ety: Pehl. khruś; Zend khruś; S. क्रोश, rt. क्रुश्
def: s.m. (f.?) Loud noise, cry, call, shout, yell; tumult; crash.
more: (proper. ḵẖurosh ) s. m. f. A crash, loud noise, tumult, cry, clamour.

def: adj. Making a loud noise, yelling, shouting; resounding.
more: (part. act. of خروشيدن) adj. Making a loud noise.

def: v.t. = kharoṅṭnā = kharoćnā, q.v. v.t. To scrape, scratch, to claw.
s.m. Seam (of a garment) which extends from the armpit down the side.
s.m. A sandal; a kind of clog or patten.
more: s. m. 1. A sandal. 2. The seam of a garment which extends from the armpit down the side. ¶ khurpā, 1. An instrument for scraping grass for horses. 2. The kneepan, patella.

def: s.m. A thief, rogue, cheat; — skull; upper part of the head; — a beggar's bowl, &c. &c. ( = khaprā, q.v.); — a collyrium or application for the eyes.
s.m. The marsh-melon, Cucumis melo.

s.m. Rough draught (of a letter, &c.), rough account, sketch; schedule; memorandum; — an iron instrument to rub horses with, a curry-comb; — an ulcer, with hardness of skin, on the back of the foot; scab, scale.
more: n. m. A schedule; memorandum of transactions; rough account.

s. m. 1. The rough draught of a letter or any writing, a sketch. 2. An iron instrument to rub horses with, a currycomb. 3. An ulcer with hardness of the skin on the back of the foot. ¶ kharā, adj. See کهڙا khaṛā.

ety: prob. corr. fr. A. ḵẖarrāt̤, q.v.
def: s.f. A lathe
more: (خراط) s. f. A lathe. خراد چڙهنا ḵẖarād chaṛhnā, To be polished (as, a clown by intercourse with the society of a city).
subs: and ḵẖarād ćaṛhnā, 'To be put on the lathe'; to be polished (by intercourse with a superior character, or with refined society).

def: s.m. A turner ( = ḵẖarrāt̤ī).
more: s. m. (dakh.) A carpenter ( Harris ), a joiner.

ety: fr. خرط 'to pull or rub off'
def: s.m. A turner (of wood, &c.); — s.f. A turner's lathe (also spelt ḵẖarāt̤).
more: s. f. A turnerʼs lathe. s. m. A turner.

ety: prob. fr. A. خر 'to sound'; or onomat.
def: s.m. Snoring (see kharrāṭā)
more: s. m. Snoring. خراٿا مارنا ḵẖarrāṭā mārnā, To snore.
subs: ḵẖarrāṭā lenā, ḵẖarrāṭā mārnā, v.n. To snore.
wety: Onomatopoeic from خَر (xar) + اٹا (āṭā). Compare Arabic خَر (ḵar, “snore, gurgle”).

ety: prob. onomat.; cf. S. खरट, खरटा
def: s.m. Snoring ( = ḵẖarrāṭā)
adj. Turned on a lathe; — s.m. A turner; — s.f. The business of a turner.
more: 1. s. m. A turner. 2. adj. Turned.
